free_text: Elements From a Comparison of 800 MeV Proton
.New Mexico State University, Las Cruces, New
Sn-120,Sm-144 and Pb-208 With Areal Densities Ranging
Proton and Neutron Deformation Lenths and
the Beam Intensity was Monitored Simultaneously by a
Set of Ion Chambers Located Inside the Scattering
Chamber and by a Set of Two Fixed Three-Fold
Coincidence Monitor Telescopes, Which Viewed the Target
at +-40 Laboratory Angular Degrees, Approximately. At
Laboratory Angles >=12 Degrees a Secondary Emission
Monitor Located Outside the Scattering Chamber, 3
Meters Downstream of the Target, was Also Used.
